g.data-narration: Welcome to JDBC Metadata and Exception Handling. Learn how to discover database structure programmatically.
g.text: Metadata and Exception Handling in JDBC

g.data-narration: Metadata means 'data about data' - information describing database structure rather than the actual data stored.
g.text: Metadata describes the "structure" of data - table names, column types, constraints,
and database capabilities. It's essential for building dynamic database tools.

g.data-narration: JDBC provides three metadata interfaces: DatabaseMetaData for database info, ResultSetMetaData for query results, and ParameterMetaData for prepared statements.
DatabaseMetaData is obtained from Connection object. It reveals database product name, version, driver info, and supported features.
Use getTables() method to discover all tables in a database. Filter by table type - typically TABLE - to get user-defined tables.
g.text: ResultSet tables = meta.getTables(null, null, "%", new String[]{"TABLE"});

g.data-narration: Use getColumns() to get column details like name, type, size, and nullable property for any table in the database.
ResultSetMetaData describes ResultSet columns - their count, names, types, and display sizes. Essential for dynamic query processing.
This example shows dynamic table display - headers and rows are built at runtime using ResultSetMetaData, not hardcoded.
ParameterMetaData provides info about PreparedStatement parameters - their count, types, and modes (IN, OUT, INOUT).
g.text: PreparedStatement ps = conn.prepareStatement("INSERT INTO emp VALUES (?, ?, ?)");

g.data-narration: JDBC exception hierarchy: SQLException is the base, with SQLWarning for warnings, BatchUpdateException for batch failures, and DataTruncation for data loss.
SQLException provides getSQLState() for standard error codes, getErrorCode() for vendor codes, and getMessage() for human-readable descriptions.
SQLWarnings don't stop execution but indicate issues. BatchUpdateException contains update counts showing which batch operations succeeded.
DataTruncation occurs when data exceeds column size. It reports dataSize (actual) vs transferSize (what was saved).
Modern Java uses try-with-resources for automatic resource cleanup. Connection, Statement, and ResultSet all implement AutoCloseable.

gclass.data-narration: Always rollback transactions on failure to maintain data integrity. Set autoCommit back to true in finally block for subsequent operations.

g.data-narration: The metadata flow: Application calls getMetaData(), which queries the database, returning metadata in a ResultSet for processing.
Key takeaways: Use DatabaseMetaData for database info, ResultSetMetaData for query columns, ParameterMetaData for prepared statements, and always handle exceptions properly.
